title: Verdrängungsimpuls und Ersetzungswert: Eine relationale Matrixmethode für das 0/1-Knapsack-Problem
description: A novel relational heuristic for the 0/1 knapsack problem based on an n×n proportional matrix. Each item is simultaneously treated as container and content. The matrix cell M[i][j] = floor(w_i/w_j) * v_j/v_i quantifies the replacement value: how much more valuable would the weight slot of item i be if filled with copies of item j? The derived core score M-bar_j (mean replacement value over all accepting containers) is parameter-free, O(n²), and independent of knapsack capacity W. The method uses the UKP dominance operator floor(w_i/w_j) — known as Simple Dominance (Kellerer et al., 2004) and Threshold Dominance (Andonov et al., 2000) — not for item elimination but for relational scoring, creating a bridge between unbounded and 0/1 knapsack theory not previously present in the literature.
